title: Schema versioning and evolution
description: Schemas have no version field, no $id, no migration story, no $ref composition, no schema reuse via $ref. Schemas drift silently across projects and across time. A schema rename or field rename has no backward-compatibility path. Schemas are effectively copy-paste snapshots with no evolution protocol.

evidence.reference: flat draft-07 schema with no $id, no version, no allOf / anyOf composition
duplicates fields from decisions.schema.json with no $ref to a shared schema — rename in one does not propagate
'All schemas are user-customizable. Edit .project/schemas/*.schema.json to add fields, change enums, or restructure — no code changes needed.' — no mention of migration or version compatibility

impact: Long-term schema evolution is undefined. Projects that adopted the framework early and never updated their .project/schemas/ drift from projects that adopted later. There is no way for tooling to know what schema version a block conforms to.
canonical_vocabulary: schema evolution; JSON Schema $id; schema registry; schema versioning
proposed_resolution:
Add a required $id (URI) and version field on every schema. schema-validator reads version and can apply migration functions. Introduce a schema-migrations registry. Schema composition via $ref becomes the standard way to express shared fields (e.g. a common 'authorship.schema.json' imported by every block schema).
